name: SSH Brute Force Detection
description: Multiple failed SSH authentication attempts from the same source IP within a short time window. This pattern typically indicates an automated password guessing attack targeting the SSH service. Common attack tools include Hydra, Medusa, and NCrack.

name: Privilege Escalation Attempt
description: Attempted use of sudo or su commands by unauthorized users, or unusual privilege escalation patterns. This could indicate an attacker attempting to gain root access after initial compromise.

name: Firewall Rule Modification
description: Changes to iptables, ufw, or other firewall configurations. Unauthorized firewall modifications could indicate an attacker attempting to open backdoor access or disable security controls.

name: Kernel Module Loading
description: Loading of kernel modules, especially unknown or unsigned modules. Rootkits often use kernel modules to hide their presence and maintain persistence.

name: Port Scan Detection
description: Multiple connection attempts to different ports from the same source IP. This reconnaissance activity typically precedes targeted attacks.
